[{"motor_ref":"05d2d324","motor_type":"MathjaxBlank","motor_json":"{\"problemText\":\"The band leader asks, \\\"What adjustments did you make, to end up with this pitch?\\\"\\n\\nClora says, \\\"We sounded out-of-tune. There was a beat about every quarter second. So I moved the slide to lengthen my trumpet and lower my pitch.\\\"\\n\\nDizzy says, \\\"Yes, and I moved my slide to shorten my trumpet and raise my pitch by the same amount, so our pitches matched.\\\"\\n\\nPrior to the adjustments, Clora and Dizzy played with a beat frequency $4.2\\\\;\\\\mathrm{Hz}\\\\,.$ Then, they both used an adjustable part of the trumpet called a \\\"slide\\\" to change the instrument length. \\n\\n\\n[center][img width=40]http:\\/\\/cdn.artofproblemsolving.com\\/school\\/crypt\\/00540-e12599cedadfdc40445dca22ddd227e6417975d2\\/files\\/11_script_trumpet_valves.png[\\/img]\\n[size=10] Trumpet valves by Trumpet Heroes, https:\\/\\/trumpetheroes.com\\/trumpet-valves\\/ [\\/size] [\\/center]\\n\\nAfter these adjustments, they played with no beating (but it was the wrong note as we saw in Part A). \\n\\n[b]What was Dizzy's original frequency? Round your answer to the nearest hertz.[\\/b]\\n\",\"barChartText\":null,\"type\":\"MathjaxBlank\",\"parseFormat\":\"values\",\"outputDisplayFormat\":\"inlineNewJax\",\"barChartFormat\":null,\"options\":{\"mathjax\":\"\\\\[\\\\blank{??????}\\\\;\\\\mathrm{Hz}\\\\;.\\\\]\",\"allAllowedCharacters\":\"1023456789\",\"maxLength\":[4]},\"problem_text_fmt\":\"The band leader asks, "What adjustments did you make, to end up with this pitch?"
\\n
\\nClora says, "We sounded out-of-tune. There was a beat about every quarter second. So I moved the slide to lengthen my trumpet and lower my pitch."
\\n
\\nDizzy says, "Yes, and I moved my slide to shorten my trumpet and raise my pitch by the same amount, so our pitches matched."
\\n
\\nPrior to the adjustments, Clora and Dizzy played with a beat frequency Then, they both used an adjustable part of the trumpet called a "slide" to change the instrument length.
\\n
\\n\\n
\\n
\\n Trumpet valves by Trumpet Heroes, https:\\/\\/trumpetheroes.com\\/trumpet-valves\\/ <\\/span>\\n<\\/div>\\n
\\nAfter these adjustments, they played with no beating (but it was the wrong note as we saw in Part A).
\\n
\\nWhat was Dizzy's original frequency? Round your answer to the nearest hertz.<\\/b>
\\n\"}"}]
solution_text:
Dizzy's frequency was $ oxed{ 228\;\mathrm{Hz}}\,.$

After the adjustments, Clora and Dizzy both play  $230\;\mathrm{Hz}\,.$

Before the adjustments, their frequencies were equal amounts above and below that value.

Also before the adjustments, $f_{	ext{beat}}=4\;\mathrm{Hz}\,.$ 

So their frequencies were $4\;\mathrm{Hz}$ apart, equal amounts above and below $230\;\mathrm{Hz}\,.$

Since \[ \dfrac{4\;\mathrm{Hz} }{2} = 2\;\mathrm{Hz}\;,\]

Dizzy's frequency was \[ 230\;\mathrm{Hz} -  2\;\mathrm{Hz} = 228\;\mathrm{Hz}\;\]
